text.title: LLM Hallucination Test Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Suite passes 100%. Production cites fabricated regulations weekly.
antipattern.root-cause: Suite covers fact_probes only; citation_verification absent.
antipattern.fix: Coverage matrix across all 6 classes; CI rejects suite with missing classes.

antipattern.symptom: Pattern with n=3 cases. One case fails. Pass-rate drops 33%. Looks like big regression, actually within noise.
antipattern.fix: Hard floor: 10 cases per pattern; promotion blocked below.

antipattern.symptom: Suite passes because judge agrees with model. Both wrong on the same axis.
antipattern.root-cause: Gold labels generated by LLM judge without human review.
antipattern.fix: Each case has human gold label + author handle + timestamp; LLM-judge labels rejected at curation.

antipattern.symptom: Two reviewers rate same response pass vs fail. Suite results irreproducible.
antipattern.root-cause: Rubric prose says 'response should be reasonable'.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite rubric per pattern with explicit pass/fail criteria; calibrate on 10 cases before opening curation.
